2 min read
2 hours ago

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Developers love grinding.
Long hours.
More coffee.
More debugging.
But here’s the uncomfortable truth:
Most bugs I’ve fixed were solved after I stepped away from the screen.
Not after another Stack Overflow search.
Not after rewriting the code for the third time.
Just five minutes away.
Why Developers Get Stuck
When you stare at code long enough, your brain stops seeing problems.
You start doing things like:

• Re-running the same test

• Re-checking the same function

• Blaming the wrong file
Your brain switches into tunnel vision mode.
The 5-Minute Reset
When I’m stuck debugging something in Node.js or Express, I do one thing:

• Close the laptop

• Walk away

• No phone

• No “thinking about the code”
Just five minutes.
That’s it.
Why It Works
Your brain keeps working in the background.
Psychologists call this incubation — your subconscious continues processing the problem.
Developers experience it as:
“Wait… why didn’t I check that earlier?”
A Real Example
I once spent 45 minutes debugging an Express route that kept returning a 500 error.
I checked:

• middleware

• database query

• validation
Nothing.
I walked away for five minutes.
When I came back, I immediately noticed the issue:
res.send(data)
next()I had accidentally called next() after sending the response.
Classic Express mistake.
Forty-five minutes wasted.
Five-minute reset solved it.
The Real Problem
Most developers think productivity means never stopping.
But the best engineers I’ve worked with do the opposite.
They pause.
They step away.
They reset.
Because sometimes the fastest way to solve a problem…
is to stop trying for a moment.
Final Thought
The next time you’re stuck debugging something stupid for 30 minutes:
Don’t open another tab.
Take a five-minute reset.
Your code might not change.
But your brain will.
